Mathematical Foundations of AI & ML
Unit 7: Bayesian Reasoning

Prof. Dr. Philipp Pelz

FAU Erlangen-Nürnberg

01. Title: Bayesian Reasoning

  • Unit scope and motivation.

02. Learning outcomes

  • Posterior reasoning and decision logic.

03. Recap from Unit 6

  • Frequentist inference bridge.

04. Bayesian worldview

  • Probability as degree of belief.

05. Prior, likelihood, posterior

  • Core decomposition.

06. Evidence (marginal likelihood)

  • Normalization and model comparison role.

07. Bayes rule revisited

  • Parameter inference form.

08. Conjugacy intuition

  • Closed-form posterior updates.

09. Beta-Bernoulli example

  • Binary outcome learning.

10. Gaussian-Gaussian example

  • Mean inference intuition.

11. Posterior predictive distribution

  • Predictive uncertainty.

12. Sequential updating

  • Learning from incoming data.

13. Influence of prior strength

  • Data-poor vs data-rich regimes.

14. Informative vs weak priors

  • Domain knowledge injection.

15. MAP vs MLE

  • Regularization connection.

16. Credible intervals

  • Bayesian uncertainty summaries.

17. CI vs credible interval

  • Interpretation contrast.

18. Decision theory framing

  • Utility-aware choices.

19. Loss functions and Bayes actions

  • Optimal decisions under posterior.

20. Model evidence intuition

  • Occam factor concept.

21. Bayes factors (conceptual)

  • Relative model support.

22. Hierarchical models (preview)

  • Partial pooling motivation.

23. Latent variable viewpoint

  • Hidden causes framing.

24. Approximate inference need

  • When exact posterior is hard.

25. MCMC intuition

  • Sampling-based approximation.

26. Variational inference intuition

  • Optimization-based approximation.

27. Calibration and Bayesian confidence

  • Reliability perspective.

28. Epistemic uncertainty handling

  • Model uncertainty benefits.

29. Bayesian linear regression (preview)

  • Closed-form uncertainty in weights.

30. Bayesian classification hint

  • Probabilistic decision boundaries.

31. Prior misspecification risks

  • Wrong assumptions consequences.

32. Sensitivity analysis

  • Check prior robustness.

33. Computational cost tradeoffs

  • Accuracy vs efficiency.

34. Bayesian vs frequentist summary

  • Practical comparison grid.

35. Materials example: sparse data regime

  • Prior-guided learning value.

36. Materials example: rare defects

  • Posterior risk-aware decisions.

37. Materials example: batch effects

  • Hierarchical modeling motivation.

38. Leakage and priors

  • Hidden information risks.

39. Reporting Bayesian results

  • Posterior plots and intervals.

40. Reproducibility checklist
